column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-appkit-interop (interop-01 … interop-10).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard → runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and decides. Structural rules that
# grep cannot express (a type MISSING a member, controller-flattened-to-view) live in ast-grep/*.yml (tier 2).

column4: becomeFirstResponder() — INVALID on a SwiftUI value; a custom focusable NSView must override acceptsFirstResponder + focus via window.makeFirstResponder (interop-03)
AppKit adding a subview / setting contentView — if it hand-instantiates a SwiftUI View, wrap it in NSHostingView/NSHostingController (interop-05). READ to confirm.

concurrency hop near a bridge — if inside/around a Coordinator reading parent/main-actor state, it is the Swift-6 "can not be referenced from a Sendable closure" error class (interop-06, hard under Swift 6). cross_ref concurrency-safety.

in-window material — if it backs a sidebar/panel posing as native vibrancy, it composites IN-window (flat); a real sidebar needs NSVisualEffectView .behindWindow (interop-07). cross_ref appearance-color.
scene chrome under hosting — .searchable has NO scene bridge under NSHostingView (any macOS); .toolbar/.navigationTitle need sceneBridgingOptions (macOS 14, [] default when nested) (interop-08)
observer/KVO/timer — if added in makeNSView with no static func dismantleNSView, it leaks across view-identity changes (interop-09). CADisplayLink requires macOS 14.0+. READ the type.

newer bridge surface — REPORT only if its floor (NSHostingMenu 14.4, NSAnimationContext.animate 15, sceneBridgingOptions/NSHostingSizingOptions/sizeThatFits 13–14) is ABOVE the deployment target and it is ungated (interop-10). Cross-check floors-master.md.

a representable type — READ it: it MUST implement both make/update halves + makeCoordinator (if @Binding) + dismantleNSView (if observers). Structural absence checks are in ast-grep (interop-01/02/04).
a reverse bridge (SwiftUI-in-AppKit) — the SANCTIONED host. Confirm it is used (not hand-instantiation) and chrome bridging is wired if needed (interop-05/08 context).
